name: Service-to-Service Contract Governance
owner: TBD — accountable McCain architect not provided in attached context
approvers: TBD — governing board and named approver not provided in attached context
strategicIntent: This Draft pattern establishes McCain guardrails for service-to-service contract governance across application & integration so teams can reuse a vetted design reference early in solution shaping. Missing items before endorsement: named McCain owner/approver, approved CCoE certification reference, LeanIX registration, and validated implementation asset links from the enterprise repository.
problemStatement: McCain applications need consistent integration mediation and contract control so ERP, MES, supply-chain, and digital products are not coupled through unmanaged point-to-point interfaces. This pattern solves the specific design decision for service-to-service contract governance.

useCases.use: Apply Service-to-Service Contract Governance when a McCain project needs a reusable, governed approach in the AP domain for production or pre-production workloads.
useCases.dontUse: Do not use where a temporary proof of concept has no enterprise data, no plant connectivity, and no path to production without later architecture review.

useCases.use: Use during vendor solution assessment to compare proposed architecture against McCain guardrails and exception requirements.
useCases.dontUse: Do not use to justify bypassing domain-specific risk assessment, data classification, or OT safety review where those reviews are required.

components: Azure API Management — API mediation, policy enforcement, and developer onboarding
Azure Service Bus or Event Hubs — asynchronous integration backbone
Schema Registry — contract and event schema version control
Azure Functions or App Service — integration runtime and business logic host
Application Insights — request tracing and dependency telemetry
interactionSteps: 1. Publish interface or integration need for service-to-service contract governance.
2. Validate contract and mediation policy for service-to-service contract governance.
3. Route traffic or events through approved service for service-to-service contract governance.
4. Observe failures and dependency health for service-to-service contract governance.
5. Version or retire contracts through governance for service-to-service contract governance.

securityConsiderations: Access must use least privilege and be traceable to an approved McCain identity, workload identity, or support process.
Any PII, employee, supplier, customer, quality, or production-sensitive data must be classified before the pattern is implemented.
Administrative actions must be logged to the central observability/SOC platform with retention aligned to the applicable data class.
API/event schemas must be versioned and validated to prevent breaking changes and unvalidated payload injection.

guardrails.rule: Production use requires private or controlled access paths; public exposure requires documented exception and compensating controls.
The workload must have owner, cost centre, data classification, environment, and criticality metadata before production approval.
Monitoring, alert routing, and operational runbook ownership must be defined before go-live.
Teams should define expected capacity, retention, and consumption limits during design review.

benefits: Gives McCain teams a common review reference for service-to-service contract governance instead of re-solving the design per project.
Improves audit readiness by documenting required controls, evidence, and exception paths early.
risks: If service-to-service contract governance is applied without data classification, criticality assessment, or domain review, teams may create false assurance and miss local plant or regulatory constraints.
constraints: Specific McCain tool URLs, CCoE-certified modules, named owners, and LeanIX references are TBD because they were not provided in the attached context.

implementationAssets.azurePolicy: TBD — approved Azure Policy for AP-002 not provided in attached context
implementationAssets.ccoeRunbook: TBD — approved CCoE runbook for AP-002 not provided in attached context

exceptionProcess: Contact the McCain Enterprise Architecture Review Board and the relevant domain architect. Submit an Architecture Decision Record, data classification, threat model, operational impact assessment, compliance impact mapping, and compensating controls through the architecture governance intake; approval is required before implementation.
